risk_if_not_implemented: Without Automatic Spam and Phishing Protection Updates, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Malicious Code Protection (Anti-Malware)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize antimalware technologies to detect and eradicate malicious code.
compensating_control_1.justification: Malicious Code Protection (Anti-Malware) (END-04) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Automatic Spam and Phishing Protection Updates (END-08.2) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Email Content Protections
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to implement an email filtering security service to detect malicious attachments in emails and prevent users from accessing them.
compensating_control_2.justification: Email Content Protections (NET-20) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Automatic Spam and Phishing Protection Updates (END-08.2) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
